initial_program:
% Colors 09
color(0).
color(1).
color(2).
color(3).
color(4).
color(5).
color(6).
color(7).
color(8).
color(9).

% Grid dimensions
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Boundary indices (max row / column)
maxRow(R) :- R = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
maxCol(C) :- C = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 4connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Zeros that are on the boundary or can be reached from the boundary
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = 0.
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), maxRow(R).
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = 0.
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), maxCol(C).

outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    R2 >= 0, R2 < NR, C2 >= 0, C2 < NC,
    input(R2,C2,0).

% Every input cell is a cell of the output
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Nonzero cells keep their original colour
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% Zero cells that are reachable must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), outside(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

% Zero cells that are not reachable become colour4
:- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 4.

rounds.program_after:
% Colors 09
color(0).
color(1).
color(2).
color(3).
color(4).
color(5).
color(6).
color(7).
color(8).
color(9).
row(R) :- input(R, _, _).
col(C) :- input(_, C, _).
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : row(R)}.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : col(C)}.

% Boundary indices (max row / column)
maxRow(R) :- row(R), n_rows(NR), R = NR.
maxCol(C) :- col(C), n_cols(NC), C = NC.

% 4connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Zeros that are on the boundary or can be reached from the boundary
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = 0.
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), maxRow(R).
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = 0.
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), maxCol(C).

outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    R2 >= 0, R2 < NR, C2 >= 0, C2 < NC,
    input(R2,C2,0).

% Every input cell is a cell of the output
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Nonzero cells keep their original colour
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% Zero cells that are reachable must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), outside(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

% Zero cells that are not reachable become colour4
:- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 4.

rounds.program_after:
% Colors 09
color(0).
color(1).
color(2).
color(3).
color(4).
color(5).
color(6).
color(7).
color(8).
color(9).
row(R) :- input(R, _, _).
col(C) :- input(_, C, _).
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : row(R)}.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : col(C)}.

% Boundary indices (max row / column)
maxRow(R) :- row(R), n_rows(NR), R = NR.
maxCol(C) :- col(C), n_cols(NC), C = NC.

% 4connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Zeros that are on the boundary or can be reached from the boundary
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = 0.
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), maxRow(R).
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = 0.
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), maxCol(C).

outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    R2 >= 0, R2 < NR, C2 >= 0, C2 < NC,
    input(R2,C2,0).

% Every input cell is a cell of the output
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Nonzero cells keep their original colour
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% Zero cells that are reachable must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), outside(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

% Zero cells that are not reachable become colour4
:- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 4.

final_program:
% Colors 09
color(0).
color(1).
color(2).
color(3).
color(4).
color(5).
color(6).
color(7).
color(8).
color(9).
row(R) :- input(R, _, _).
col(C) :- input(_, C, _).
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : row(R)}.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : col(C)}.

% Boundary indices (max row / column)
maxRow(R) :- row(R), n_rows(NR), R = NR.
maxCol(C) :- col(C), n_cols(NC), C = NC.

% 4connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Zeros that are on the boundary or can be reached from the boundary
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = 0.
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), maxRow(R).
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = 0.
outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), maxCol(C).

outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    R2 >= 0, R2 < NR, C2 >= 0, C2 < NC,
    input(R2,C2,0).

% Every input cell is a cell of the output
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Nonzero cells keep their original colour
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% Zero cells that are reachable must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), outside(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

% Zero cells that are not reachable become colour4
:- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 4.
